title: Tangy Juicy Injera Puff Pastry Crumble
description: A tangy and juicy monsoon-inspired Ethiopian dish featuring puff pastry crumbles over injera bread for a unique breakfast or snack option, low-sodium friendly.

ingredients.name: Injera Bread
ingredients.notes: Thawed or cold from the fridge

ingredients.name: Low-sodium tomato sauce (optional)
ingredients.notes: To make the dish tangier and juicier without adding salt

instructions: Thaw injera bread according to package instructions or serve it cold.
Place puff pastry sheets on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Using a sharp knife, carefully cut each sheet into six triangles and slightly press the edges down with your fingers for easier shaping.
In a pan over medium heat, sauté chopped onion until translucent, then add minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Stir in diced tomatoes, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 5 minutes. Stir occasionally; adjust seasoning if necessary (e.g., with lemon juice or low-sodium tomato sauce).
Fold puff pastry triangles in half at the top edge towards the center, then fold down one-third of each sheet to create a crumbly layer over the tomato filling.
Place filled sheets on an oven-safe plate and bake for 12-15 minutes until golden brown. Garnish with fresh mint leaves before serving hot.
Serve immediately with sour milk or yogurt for added flavor, if desired.
